title: Visa Rapid Dispute Resolution (RDR) configuration
description: RDR is Visa's fast-lane to close pre-chargeback disputes: instead of fighting through the full chargeback flow, the merchant pre-agrees to auto-refund below a configured amount. Cheapest defense (no network fee, no rate impact). Eligibility rules + thresholds captured here so RdrHandler + admin UI stay consistent.

eligibility.customer_condition: customer has >= tenant.chargeback.rdr.min_customer_purchase_history_days days of purchase history (default 30d)
eligibility.notes: All 5 conditions must be true for RDR eligibility. rdr_eligible flag is computed at chargeback creation + frozen.

thresholds.notes: Default threshold = $50. Tenant may configure 1-1000 dollars. Below $50 = auto-refund is cheaper than the ~$25 network fee + rate impact. Above $50 = merchant should typically fight; RDR becomes uneconomical.

rdr_path.notes: Merchant loses the disputed amount but avoids the network fee + rate impact. Cheapest path for low-value disputes.

fight_and_win.notes: Money returned + network fee retained by bank + counts toward VDMP. Best outcome IF you win, but win rate baseline is 15-30% for fraud disputes.

fight_and_lose.notes: Worst outcome — you lose the disputed amount AND pay the network fee AND increment the VDMP rate.

expired_auto_loss.notes: Same as fight_and_lose. Never let evidence deadline pass — always at minimum accept via RDR when eligible.

amount_lte_5000.rationale: Loss ($50) < network fee ($15-25) + rate impact. Accept.

amount_5000_to_25000.rationale: Amount worth fighting for. Submit auto-collected evidence.

amount_gt_25000_fraud_reason.rationale: High-value fraud dispute. CE3.0 evidence dramatically improves win rate (75% vs 15% baseline).

amount_gt_25000_consumer_dispute.rationale: Service documentation (attendance records, communication logs, refund policy snapshot) drives win rate for consumer disputes.

amount_gt_25000_processing_error.rationale: Some processing errors (duplicate_processing) always favor cardholder — accept via refund. Others (late_presentment) winnable with clear records.

operational_notes: Visa enrolls merchants in RDR by application. Merchants must apply through their acquirer (Stripe, Adyen, etc.) to be enrolled — this module ASSUMES enrollment is in place for tenants holding chargeback_rdr entitlement.

RDR pre-agrees to auto-refund; the merchant does NOT click accept once the chargeback is filed — the refund fires automatically pre-chargeback. Our RDR path is admin-initiated (POST /chargebacks/{chargeback}/accept) because we track the RDR outcome + create the refund + close the chargeback within our system.

Some Visa transactions may auto-refund via RDR BEFORE we see a chargeback (Visa auto-refunds and never files) — in that case, the chargeback module never sees the event. Only chargebacks that DO get filed pass through this module.
Mastercard's equivalent to RDR is 'Dispute Resolution 2.0' — different mechanics + not currently supported by this module. Extend when Mastercard DR2 support lands (Wave 5+).
